heading: Arctic maritime transportation
label: 716 Arctic maritime transportation

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: a .—Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to ensure safe and secure maritime shipping in the Arctic including the availability of aids to navigation, vessel escorts, spill response capability, and maritime search and rescue in the Arctic.
placement and maintenance of aids to navigation;
appropriate marine safety, tug, and salvage capabilities;
oil spill prevention and response capability;
maritime domain awareness, including long-range vessel tracking; and

children.heading: .—Coordination by Committee on the Maritime Transportation System
children.label: c .—Coordination by Committee on the Maritime Transportation System
children.text: The Committee on the Maritime Transportation System established under section 55501 of title 46, United States Code, shall coordinate the establishment of domestic transportation policies in the Arctic necessary to carry out the purpose of this section.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Agreements and Contracts
children.label: d .—Agreements and Contracts
children.text: The Secretary may, subject to the availability of appropriations, enter into cooperative agreements, contracts, or other agreements with, or make grants to, individuals and governments to carry out the purpose of this section or any agreements established under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Icebreaking
children.label: e .—Icebreaking
children.text: The Secretary shall promote safe maritime navigation by means of icebreaking where necessary, feasible, and effective to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: .—Arctic Definition
children.label: f .—Arctic Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “Arctic” has the meaning given such term in section 112 of the Arctic Research and Policy Act of 1984 ().15 U.S.C. 4111
